title: Email Template Service Revamp

summary.overview: This PR refactors the email template service from a monolithic string-concatenation approach to a Razor-based templating engine with layout inheritance and reusable components. The migration preserves all existing templates while introducing a structured authoring model for new ones. Code quality is high with good separation between template rendering and email delivery concerns.

riskAssessment.summary: The refactoring introduces a new rendering engine alongside the existing one with an adapter pattern for backward compatibility. The legacy string-based templates continue to work unchanged, and the new Razor engine is opt-in per template, minimizing blast radius.
items: Razor template compilation occurs at runtime, which could introduce latency on first render if templates are not pre-compiled
The legacy adapter maps old template placeholders to Razor model properties, and any unmapped placeholder will silently render as empty

strengths: Adapter pattern for legacy templates ensures zero disruption to existing email flows during the migration period
Template resolver supports multiple lookup strategies including convention-based, explicit path, and embedded resource fallback
Layout inheritance implementation is clean and supports nested layouts up to a configurable depth to prevent infinite recursion
Comprehensive test suite validates rendering output for all existing production templates against their expected HTML baselines

issues.message: Compiled templates are cached in a static ConcurrentDictionary without any eviction policy, which will grow unbounded if template variants are generated dynamically with unique keys.
issues.recommendation: Introduce a bounded cache with an LRU eviction policy or use IMemoryCache with size limits and sliding expiration. Add a metric to track cache hit rates and size growth in production.

issues.message: Unmapped placeholders in legacy templates are silently replaced with empty strings, which could cause customer-facing emails to render with missing personalization data without any alert.
issues.recommendation: Log a warning when an unmapped placeholder is encountered and consider adding a validation step that checks all placeholders in a template have corresponding model properties before rendering.

recommendations: Add a startup pre-compilation step for all registered Razor templates to eliminate first-render latency in production
Implement a template preview endpoint that renders templates with sample data for QA verification before deployment
Create a migration guide for the team documenting how to convert existing string-based templates to the new Razor format
